frameworks.name: deep convolutional autoencoder with embedded clustering
frameworks.one_line: joint feature learning and clustering on fused functional + structural fiber descriptors

frameworks.name: tract-based analysis of white matter
frameworks.one_line: downstream goal motivating fiber clustering of tractography output

core_question: Can whole-brain white-matter fibers be clustered into bundles that are both structurally and functionally meaningful by jointly encoding tractography and task-fMRI signals?
key_assumptions: fiber similarity should reflect both anatomy and function, not anatomy alone
task-fMRI activation along a fiber carries information about its functional role
a deep autoencoder can learn a more discriminative embedding than handcrafted geometric features
manipulations: input representation: structural-only vs combined functional + structural features
use of embedded clustering layer inside the autoencoder
key_findings: method clusters whole-brain fibers into bundles described as functionally and structurally meaningful
fusing task-fMRI with tractography geometry improves cluster interpretability over structure-only approaches
interpretations: combining functional and structural cues inside a deep clustering pipeline yields bundles better suited for brain structure-function analysis
